top.title: Bowen: Trump has called for an Iran uprising but the lessons from Iraq in 1991 loom large
top.summary: The US president might learn that starting wars is much easier than ending them, writes the BBC's international editor.

top.title: Countries agree to record release of emergency oil reserves as prices surge
top.summary: The G7 group of nations welcomes the idea of releasing oil in response to the surge in prices since the US-Israel war with Iran began

top.title: 'Even under missiles we carry on living' - how young Iranians are coping with war
top.summary: Iranians say they are sheltering at home and rarely venturing out on near-empty streets as the US-Israeli bombing campaign continues.

top.title: 'Icky and heartbreaking': The $2 per hour worker behind the OnlyFans boom
top.summary: The BBC talks to a Philippines-based woman paid to pretend to be an OnlyFans star in online chats.

top.title: Meta urged to boost oversight of fake AI videos
top.summary: Meta's advisers say its methods for policing AI videos are inadequate, especially at times of crisis.

top.title: Fortnite-maker raising in-game currency prices 'to help pay the bills'
top.summary: Epic Games said it had to increase the price as the cost of running the game had "gone up a lot"

title.#text: Grammarly says it will stop using AI to clone experts without permission
Microsoft’s ‘Xbox mode’ is coming to every Windows 11 PC
Microsoft’s next Xbox, Project Helix, won’t reach alpha until 2027
